title: History & Approaches - Fill in the Blank

questions.question: Cognitive neuroscience is the intersection of cognitive science and ___.
questions.blank_sentence: Cognitive neuroscience is the intersection of cognitive science and ___.
questions.explanation: Cognitive neuroscience sits at the intersection of cognitive science (the study of mental processes) and neuroscience (the study of the nervous system).

questions.question: Franz Joseph Gall is associated with the pseudoscience of ___.
questions.blank_sentence: Franz Joseph Gall is associated with the pseudoscience of ___.
questions.explanation: Gall founded phrenology, which incorrectly claimed that mental faculties could be determined by reading bumps on the skull. While the method was wrong, the idea of functional localisation proved prescient.

questions.question: The ___ doctrine established that neurons are discrete individual cells, not a continuous network.
questions.blank_sentence: The ___ doctrine established that neurons are discrete individual cells, not a continuous network.
questions.explanation: The Neuron Doctrine, championed by Cajal using Golgi staining, established that the nervous system is made up of discrete individual cells (neurons) rather than a continuous reticulum.

questions.question: Behaviourists like Skinner treated the mind as a '___ ___', focusing only on observable stimulus-response relationships.
questions.blank_sentence: Behaviourists like Skinner treated the mind as a '___ ___', focusing only on observable stimulus-response relationships.
questions.explanation: Behaviourism deliberately avoided studying internal mental processes, treating the mind as an unknowable 'black box' and focusing only on measurable inputs (stimuli) and outputs (responses).

questions.question: Penfield mapped the somatosensory and motor cortex, creating the famous distorted figure known as the ___.
questions.blank_sentence: Penfield mapped the somatosensory and motor cortex, creating the famous distorted figure known as the ___.
questions.explanation: Penfield's homunculus is a distorted representation of the human body mapped onto the cortical surface, showing that areas like the hands and lips have disproportionately large cortical representation.

questions.question: The ___ is a brain structure critically involved in memory formation.
questions.blank_sentence: The ___ is a brain structure critically involved in memory formation.
questions.explanation: The hippocampus is a key medial temporal lobe structure essential for the formation of new declarative memories.

questions.question: Chemical messengers released at the synapse are called ___.
questions.blank_sentence: Chemical messengers released at the synapse are called ___.
questions.explanation: Neurotransmitters are chemical substances released from the presynaptic terminal into the synaptic cleft, where they bind to receptors on the postsynaptic neuron to transmit signals.

questions.question: The ___ is an almond-shaped structure involved in processing emotions, particularly fear.
questions.blank_sentence: The ___ is an almond-shaped structure involved in processing emotions, particularly fear.
questions.explanation: The amygdala is a key structure in the limbic system that plays a central role in emotional processing, especially fear conditioning and threat detection.

questions.question: The ___ acts as a relay station, routing sensory information to the appropriate areas of the cerebral cortex.
questions.blank_sentence: The ___ acts as a relay station, routing sensory information to the appropriate areas of the cerebral cortex.
questions.explanation: The thalamus is a central hub that receives sensory input (except smell) and relays it to the relevant cortical areas for processing.

questions.question: Brodmann and Semendeferi contributed to understanding the comparative anatomy of the ___ cortex across species.
questions.blank_sentence: Brodmann and Semendeferi contributed to understanding the comparative anatomy of the ___ cortex across species.
questions.explanation: Brodmann's cytoarchitectonic maps and Semendeferi's comparative studies examined how the prefrontal cortex varies across primate species, informing our understanding of human cognitive specialisation.
